title: Hack The Box Platform Rules
text:
# Hack The Box Platform Rules

> **Source:** Hack The Box Help Center
> **Last Updated:** Approximately 2 weeks ago
> **Purpose:** Rules and Terms of Service governing the use of the Hack The Box platform.

---

title: 1. Permitted and Prohibited Hacking Targets
text: # 1. Permitted and Prohibited Hacking Targets

title: ✅ Permitted
text:
## ✅ Permitted

- Hack **only** the specific target IP address or entry point provided by Hack The Box.
- Limit all testing strictly to the assigned machine or lab environment.

title: ❌ Prohibited
text:
## ❌ Prohibited

- Do **not** attack unintended machines on the HTB network.
 - Example:
 - Network gateways
 - Other infrastructure
 - Non-target nodes (e.g. `10.10.10.0/24`)
- Do **not** attack other Hack The Box users.
- If you accidentally compromise an unintended machine:
 1. Stop immediately.
 2. Do not modify or damage the system.
 3. Report the incident to the HTB team.

---

title: 2. Prohibited Attack Types
text: # 2. Prohibited Attack Types

title: ❌ Denial of Service (DoS)
text:
## ❌ Denial of Service (DoS)

The following are strictly forbidden:

- DoS attacks
- Distributed DoS (DDoS)
- Resource exhaustion attacks
- Any attack intended to make systems unavailable

If a DoS occurs accidentally:

1. Stop immediately.
2. Notify the Hack The Box team.

---

title: 3. System Security Recommendations
text:
# 3. System Security Recommendations

Hack The Box strongly recommends:

- Use a dedicated Virtual Machine (VM) for HTB.
- Avoid connecting from your primary personal computer.
- Consider using:
 - Kali Linux VM
 - Dedicated physical machine
 - Separate penetration testing workstation

### Personal Data

HTB states that:

- The network contains highly skilled security researchers.
- Users are responsible for protecting their own systems.
- HTB is not responsible for:
 - Data loss
 - Theft
 - Damage to personal devices

HTB will cooperate with authorities if necessary.

---

title: 4. Content Sharing Policy
text: # 4. Content Sharing Policy

title: Allowed Content
text:
## Allowed Content

The following content may be published, streamed, or uploaded:

- Retired Machines
- Retired Sherlocks
- Retired Challenges
- Starting Point Machines
- Tier 0 Academy Modules
- Mini Pro Labs with official write-ups:
 - POO
 - Xen
 - Ascension
 - RPG
 - Hades

---

title: Prohibited Content
text:
## Prohibited Content

Do **not** publish or stream solutions for:

- Active Machines
- Active Challenges
- Active Sherlocks
- Active Pro Labs
- Mini Pro Labs without official write-ups

---

title: Spoilers
text:
## Spoilers

Do not share or disclose solutions for active HTB content with other members.

---

title: Terms of Service Violations
text:
## Terms of Service Violations

Publishing prohibited material may result in:

- Account action
- Removal requests
- Legal action by Hack The Box

---

title: 5. Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Restrictions
text:
# 5. Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Restrictions

Hack The Box explicitly prohibits using HTB content for AI or machine learning purposes.

The following are **not permitted**:

- Training machine learning models
- Fine-tuning LLMs
- Evaluating AI models using HTB content
- Benchmarking AI systems
- Developing AI systems using HTB content
- Building datasets from HTB content
- Creating knowledge bases from HTB content
- Generating synthetic data derived from HTB intellectual property

---

title: Compliance Checklist
text: # Compliance Checklist

title: Allowed
text:
## Allowed

- Hack only assigned HTB targets
- Use dedicated testing systems
- Publish retired content only
- Report accidental compromises
- Report accidental DoS

title: Not Allowed
text:
## Not Allowed

- Attack unintended HTB systems
- Attack other HTB users
- Perform DoS attacks
- Publish active machine write-ups
- Share active machine solutions
- Train or fine-tune AI using HTB content
- Build AI datasets from HTB content
- Create knowledge bases from HTB content

title: legal_index — chunk 1
text:
# Hack The Box Legal & Compliance Index

**Organization:** Hack The Box 
**Category:** Policy Database 
**Source:** https://www.hackthebox.com/legal 
**Purpose:** Index of official Hack The Box legal, privacy, compliance, and community documents used by the APEX PolicyAdvisor.

---

# Overview

This directory contains the official legal and compliance documentation published by Hack The Box.

These documents define the legal, ethical, privacy, acceptable-use, community, and contractual rules governing use of the Hack The Box platform.

The PolicyAdvisor retrieves information from these documents to determine whether a planned action complies with Hack The Box policies before execution.

---

# Customer Terms

| Document | File |
|----------|------|
| Terms of Service for Companies | `htb_terms_of_service_for_companies.pdf` |
| Acceptable Use Policy | `htb_acceptable_use_policy.pdf` |
| End User Subscription Agreement | `htb_end_user_subscription_agreement.pdf` |
| Service Levels Agreement | `htb_service_levels_agreement.pdf` |
| User Agreement | `htb_user_agreement.pdf` |

---

# Security & Privacy

| Document | File |
|----------|------|
| Privacy Notice | `htb_privacy_notice.pdf` |
| Candidate Privacy Notice | `htb_candidate_privacy_notice.pdf` |
| Data Processing Agreement | `htb_data_processing_agreement.pdf` |
| Data Security Measures | `htb_data_security_measures.pdf` |
| Processors List (Individuals) | `htb_processors_list_individuals.pdf` |
| Sub Processors List (C

title: legal_index — chunk 2
text:
ompanies) | `htb_sub_processors_list_companies.pdf` |

---

# Partners & Community

| Document | File |
|----------|------|
| One-Time Reseller Agreement | `htb_one_time_reseller_agreement.pdf` |
| Submission Terms | `htb_submission_terms.pdf` |
| Statement of Work (SOW) Template | `htb_sow_template.pdf` |
| Reward Program Terms & Conditions | `htb_reward_program_terms_and_conditions.pdf` |

---

# Related Policy Documents

| Document | File |
|----------|------|
| HTB Platform Rules | `htb_platform_rules.md` |

---

# Intended Usage

These documents are used by the APEX PolicyAdvisor to:

- Validate whether an action is within the authorized scope.
- Verify compliance with Hack The Box Terms of Service.
- Check Acceptable Use Policy restrictions.
- Validate publication and disclosure rules.
- Enforce AI and machine learning usage restrictions.
- Reference privacy and security obligations.
- Support policy-aware autonomous penetration testing.

---

# Maintenance

The documents in this directory are official Hack The Box publications and should not be modified manually.

When Hack The Box updates a policy:

1. Download the updated official document.
2. Replace the corresponding PDF.
3. Regenerate the compiled policy database.

---

# Last Reviewed

Source: Hack The Box Legal & Compliance Portal

https://www.hackthebox.com/legal
